info.name: Cycode - Assign Tenant-Level Members to a Project
info.description:
Steps to identify existing members with unassigned tenant-level access and move them into a specific project.

Note: SSO users do not appear in the GET /v4/members response. Use Step 4 for non-SSO users and Step 5 for SSO users.

variable.description: Found in Cycode UI under Profile > Personal Access Tokens.
Found in Cycode UI under Profile > Personal Access Tokens.

exec: console.log('Token saved to collection variable token.');
console.warn('Could not find token in response. Keys: ' + Object.keys(jsonData).join(', '));

request.description:
Authenticates with Cycode using your client ID and secret. The token is automatically saved to the collection variable `token`.

Your credentials are in the Cycode UI under Profile > Personal Access Tokens.

Returns all non-SSO members in the tenant.

To identify unassigned tenant-level users, look in each user's metadata field for:
- project_ids: [] (empty — not assigned to any project)
- has_full_access: true (sitting at full tenant-level access)

For each unassigned user, note their external_id — you will need it in Step 4.

Note: SSO users do not appear in this response. Add them directly by email in Step 5.

query.description: Replace with the name of the project you want to assign members to.

request.description:
Finds the project you want to assign members to. Replace the query value with your project name.

Save the `id` from the matching item in the response — you will need it in Steps 4 and 5.

item.name: Step 4 — Assign non-SSO members to project

request.description:
Assigns existing non-SSO members to the project using their external_id values from Step 2.

Replace YOUR_PROJECT_ID_HERE in the URL with the project id from Step 3.
Replace the external_id values with those from the unassigned users in Step 2.
Add one entry per user. A 204 No Content response means success.

item.name: Step 5 — Assign SSO members to project

request.description:
Assigns existing SSO members to the project by email. SSO users do not appear in GET /v4/members so they must be added this way.

Replace YOUR_PROJECT_ID_HERE in the URL with the project id from Step 3.
Replace email and name with the SSO user's details. Add one object per user.
skip_invite: true prevents a new invitation email from being sent since they already have an account.
A 204 No Content response means success.

Valid role values: Viewer, Developer, Member, ProjectManager, Admin, Owner
